meta.title: Security & Privacy — Aethyr
meta.description: How Aethyr protects your Unreal Engine project: no telemetry, read-only by default, automatic backups, compile gate.

article.heading: Security & Privacy
article.lead: What Aethyr can and cannot do to your project, and the architecture that keeps your work safe.

sections.heading: No telemetry
sections.html:
Aethyr collects zero telemetry. There are no analytics calls, no crash reporters, no usage metrics, and no license pings. The server makes no outbound network connections of any kind during normal operation.

Your workflow data, Blueprint contents, and project structure never leave your machine.

sections.heading: Read-only by default
sections.html:
Out of the box, Aethyr is a read-only tool. All 32 read tools work immediately. Write operations—adding variables, wiring nodes, saving assets—are gated behind an environment variable:

AETHYR_ALLOW_WRITE=1

Without this variable set, any write request returns an error before touching any file. Set it only in the sessions where you want edits to happen.

sections.heading: Automatic backup ring
sections.html:
Before any write operation touches an asset, Aethyr creates a timestamped backup copy in a .aethyr-backups/ directory adjacent to the asset. The backup ring stores the last N copies per asset and rotates automatically.

If something goes wrong after a write, restore any asset by copying the backup back. The backup predates the write by design—it is always a clean copy of the pre-edit state.

No writes happen without a backup. The backup step is not optional and cannot be skipped.

sections.heading: Compile gate
sections.html:
Every write operation that modifies a Blueprint graph runs a compile check before saving. If the result would produce a compile error, the save is aborted and an error is returned to your AI client. The asset on disk is never written in this case.

This means Aethyr cannot save a broken Blueprint. A tool call either produces a clean, compiling, saved asset—or it produces nothing and tells you why.

sections.heading: Local only
sections.html:
Aethyr is a local server that runs on your development machine. It communicates with your AI client over a local stdio pipe—the same machine, no network hop. It communicates with the Unreal Editor over a local socket on loopback (127.0.0.1).

There is no Aethyr cloud service, no API key required, and no account to create. The server binary is a self-contained executable with no external runtime dependencies beyond the Unreal Editor itself.

Studio developers: Aethyr is safe to use on NDA projects. Your project data is physically incapable of leaving your machine because the server has no network egress path.
